name: Peter's Cream of Pumpkin Soup
recipeInstructions:
This Thanksgiving soup has only 65 calories per serving.
In a large saucepan, heat the oil over low heat. Add the vegetables (including the fresh pumpkin, but not the canned), thyme and bay leaf. Reduce the heat, cover, and let cook gently for 8 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent the vegetables from browning. Add the broth and the pumpkin if using canned; bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for either 20 minutes (fresh pumpkin) or 10 minutes (canned pumpkin). Puree the mixture in batches in a blender. Pour the puréed soup back into the saucepan and return to a boil. If it seems too thick, thin with a little hot broth or water. (This can be made up to this point up to 2 days in advance and refrigerated, loosely covered.) Before serving, reheat the soup to boiling. Pour back into the blender and add the cottage cheese and powdered milk. Blend for 20 seconds. Taste for seasoning. Ladle the soup into 4 heated bowls; sprinkle 2 tablespoons of the minced fine herbs over each. Serve immediately.
